label: External retrieval & ranking

node.label: External retrieval & ranking
node.definition: Relevant records, documents, examples, or tools are selected from an external collection.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, External retrieval & ranking is modeled as depending on Databases & information retrieval, Embeddings & distributed representations and feeding downstream nodes such as Episodic memory, Sparse lexical retrieval, Dense vector retrieval.
examples: atlas downstream link to Episodic memory
atlas downstream link to Sparse lexical retrieval
atlas downstream link to Dense vector retrieval
limitations: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Databases & information retrieval, Embeddings & distributed representations.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, External retrieval & ranking has a `uses` dependency on Databases & information retrieval because Databases & information retrieval is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, External retrieval & ranking has a `uses` dependency on Embeddings & distributed representations because Embeddings & distributed representations is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, External retrieval & ranking has a `uses` dependency on Datasets, sampling & splits because Datasets, sampling & splits is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, External retrieval & ranking has a `uses` dependency on Margin, ranking & metric objectives because Margin, ranking & metric objectives is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
